Title: The regulatory role of CARBON STARVED ANTHER mediated photoperiod-dependent male fertility in rice

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports anther sink strength, pollen development, and male fertility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in sink tissues such as roots and anthers; reduced in csa-SD anthers; partially restored in csa-LD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR for CSA promoter binding; dual-LUC activation assay; CRISPR/Cas9 double mutant phenotyping; sugar/starch profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes sucrose cleavage and monosaccharide supply in anthers; loss contributes to partial male sterility and reduced starch accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CWIN6 is a direct downstream target of CSA in anthers. 2. Its expression is strongly reduced in csa-SD and partially restored in csa-LD. 3. cwin6 inv4 double mutants show partial pollen sterility and altered sugar partitioning, with lower glucose/fructose and more sucrose in anthers.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target to improve anther carbohydrate allocation and stabilize fertility in PGMS systems, likely best combined with CSA or parallel sugar transport genes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutant causes partial male sterility and altered carbohydrate partitioning
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of eight direct CSA targets validated in this study; reproductive tissue-expressed.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports sugar partitioning to anther and pollen fertility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in sink tissues such as roots and anthers; downregulated in csa-SD; increased in csa-LD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; CRISPR/Cas9 double mutant; sugar/starch profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes monosaccharide production from sucrose in anthers and supports male fertility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. INV4 is directly activated by CSA. 2. Expression decreases in csa-SD and rises in csa-LD. 3. cwin6 inv4 double mutants are partially male sterile and accumulate sucrose while losing glucose/fructose in anthers.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for fertility stabilization by reinforcing sink strength in reproductive tissues
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of function impairs fertility and sugar balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included among sugar-metabolism genes enriched in sterility/restoration DEG sets.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: palea/lemma to anther transport route; anther
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports sugar transport for anther development and likely cell wall precursor delivery
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in sink tissues such as roots and anthers; reduced in csa-SD; elevated in csa-LD relative to csa-SD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; CRISPR/Cas9 triple mutant phenotyping; sugar/starch profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports sugar import/partitioning toward anthers; contributes to pollen fertility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWEET5 is a direct target of CSA. 2. sweet5 sweet6a sweet6b triple mutants show partial male sterility. 3. Sugar accumulates abnormally outside anthers while anther monosaccharides and starch decrease, indicating defective transport.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: promising transporter target for improving reproductive sugar allocation, likely requiring redundancy-aware stacking with SWEET6A/6B
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: redundant loss reduces pollen fertility and perturbs source-sink sugar distribution
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes SWEET5 was previously reported as a galactose transporter.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports sugar transport and pollen development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in roots/anthers; reduced in csa-SD; partially restored in csa-LD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; CRISPR/Cas9 triple mutant; sugar profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes sugar partitioning to anther; contributes redundantly to male fertility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWEET6A promoter is bound and activated by CSA. 2. Expression is reduced in csa-SD anthers. 3. Together with SWEET5 and SWEET6B, it is required for normal pollen fertility and sugar allocation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: can be stacked with other CSA targets to improve photoperiod-sensitive fertility systems
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: triple loss causes partial sterility and altered sugar partitioning
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated as one of the eight direct CSA targets.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports sugar transport and male fertility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in roots/anthers; near WT-SD level in csa-LD; reduced in csa-SD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; CRISPR/Cas9 triple mutant; sugar profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports sugar transport into developing anther tissues
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWEET6B is directly regulated by CSA. 2. Its expression is strongly compromised in csa-SD but recovers in csa-LD. 3. Triple SWEET mutant plants show partial pollen sterility and reduced anther monosaccharide/starch accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for engineering reproductive sugar transport under photoperiod-sensitive conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression in csa-LD approached WT-SD in RT-qPCR.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports monosaccharide transport to anther for pollen development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in sink tissues such as roots and anthers; reduced in csa-SD; elevated in csa-LD versus csa-SD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; CRISPR/Cas9 triple mutant phenotyping; sugar/starch profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports monosaccharide delivery to anthers; contributes to starch deposition and fertility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MST1 is directly targeted by CSA. 2. mst1 stp14 stp18 triple mutants alter sugar distribution, with glucose/fructose accumulation in palea/lemma and reduced anther sugar/starch. 3. Fertility defect is mild, suggesting partial redundancy.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful component for engineering sink strength, likely in combination with invertases and SWEETs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: triple loss perturbs sugar distribution though fertility remains close to WT
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of reproductive-tissue direct target set of CSA.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in roots and anthers; reduced in csa-SD; partially recovered in csa-LD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; CRISPR/Cas9 triple mutant; sugar/starch profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes anther monosaccharide supply and starch accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STP14 is a direct CSA target. 2. Expression declines in csa-SD and increases under LD restoration conditions. 3. Triple mst1 stp14 stp18 mutants show defective sugar movement to anthers.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: supports breeding strategies aimed at stronger reproductive sink activity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding by CSA confirmed by ChIP-qPCR.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: japonica; csa mutant and WT background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports monosaccharide transport for pollen maturation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in roots and anthers; down in csa-SD; higher in csa-LD than csa-SD
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; CRISPR/Cas9 triple mutant; sugar/starch profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports sugar delivery into anther sink tissues
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STP18 is directly activated by CSA. 2. It is among the sugar transport genes suppressed in csa-SD. 3. Combined disruption with MST1 and STP14 impairs sugar partitioning to anthers.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate component in multi-gene engineering of reproductive carbon allocation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of eight direct downstream targets validated in this work.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports cell wall biosynthesis associated with fertile anther development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: hub gene in turquoise module correlated with WT fertility
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with WT fertility and likely supports cell wall biosynthesis downstream of CSA-controlled network
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os12g25690 is a turquoise-module hub gene linked to WT fertility. 2. It encodes a putative UDP-glucose 6-dehydrogenase involved in cell wall precursor formation. 3. The paper implicates it in CSA-regulated anther development but does not provide direct functional genetics.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential network biomarker for fertility-associated cell wall metabolism, but requires validation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a hub gene rather than direct target.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: O. sativa ssp. japonica; csa mutant and WT
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glucose; fructose; starch; cell wall carbohydrate supply
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: maintains sugar partitioning, cell wall biosynthesis, pollen development, and male fertility
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: extremely low in leaves and very high in anthers; higher transcription under LD than SD
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in daytime and nighttime anthers; not prominent in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype comparison under SD/LD; RNA-seq; WGCNA; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR with CSA-FLAG; dual-LUC transactivation; downstream CRISPR mutant validation; sugar and starch profiling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CWIN6; INV4; SWEET5; SWEET6A; SWEET6B; MST1; STP14; STP18; MST8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates transcription of sugar metabolism and transport genes in reproductive tissues
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via promoter binding to MYB motifs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sugar partitioning, carbohydrate metabolism, sink strength, and cell wall synthesis during anther development
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: photoperiod-derived signals transduced from leaves to anthers; long-day conditions elevate CSA transcription
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential anther-specific regulator whose loss under SD causes male sterility by collapsing sugar transport/metabolism and cell wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes anther monosaccharide supply, starch accumulation, and male fertility; csa is sterile in SD and semi-fertile in LD
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSA is the central regulator controlling photoperiod-dependent male fertility in rice anthers rather than in leaves. 2. It directly binds and activates eight reproductive-tissue genes involved in sugar metabolism and transport, plus previously known target MST8. 3. Loss of CSA under SD leads to severe downregulation of sugar partitioning and cell wall genes, reduced anther glucose/fructose and starch, and complete male sterility; LD partially restores fertility through CSA homologs/other MYBs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value target for controlling PGMS fertility in two-line hybrid rice; engineering should consider photoperiod responsiveness and possible stacking with CSA2 or direct sugar transporter targets
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss causes complete sterility under SD; effects are largely reproductive-tissue specific with limited leaf transcriptional impact
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously identified as CARBON STARVED ANTHER; study indicates LD-dependent partial compensation by homologous MYB factors.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports sugar transport and male fertility under long-day conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: implicated under LD conditions as compensatory regulator
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: inference from overlap/restoration DEG analysis and cited prior report
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: common downstream sugar transport/metabolism genes with CSA
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: likely activates partial restoration targets under LD
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: sugar transport and anther fertility restoration
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent compensatory regulator for partial fertility restoration under LD
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: partially restores fertility-related sugar and cell wall processes under LD when CSA function is absent
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSA2 is discussed as a CSA homolog acting under LD conditions. 2. The paper uses transcriptome evidence to support that CSA homologs or other MYB TFs induce 218 restoration genes in csa-LD anthers. 3. Direct experimentation on CSA2 was not performed in this paper, so evidence here is indirect.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential complementary regulator for stabilizing fertility in PGMS breeding under LD environments
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: activation under LD only partially restores WT fertility
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper presents it as a mechanistic substitute in discussion, but it is not a primary experimental target in Results.
